description: You are a post-production mastering engineer. Raw materials for a 90-second documentary are in /home/ga/Videos/source/: main_video.mov (1920x1080, scratch audio to discard), narration_en.wav and narration_es.wav (NOT time-aligned with video), music_effects.wav (stereo M&E, correctly aligned), subs_en.srt and subs_es.srt (both have timing errors — see sync_notes.txt for correction details). Each audio file and the video contain a sync marker in the first 10 seconds: the video has a half-second white flash, each audio has a half-second 1kHz tone burst. Determine each narration track's timing offset by finding its beep position relative to the video's flash. The M&E track is pre-aligned. Read /home/ga/Documents/sync_notes.txt for synchronization methodology and /home/ga/Documents/delivery_spec.json for detailed output requirements. Create all outputs in /home/ga/Videos/mastered/: (1) documentary_master.mkv — 30fps H.264 video + 3 sync-corrected audio tracks (EN/ES narration, M&E) + 2 timing-corrected subtitle tracks (EN/ES) with ISO 639 language tags, (2) dist_english.mp4 — 1280x720 H.264, EN narration mixed with M&E, EN subtitles hard-burned, (3) dist_spanish.mp4 — 1280x720 H.264, ES narration mixed with M&E, ES subtitles hard-burned, (4) dist_audio.m4a — EN narration mixed with M&E, no video, (5) qa_proof_sheet.png — 3x2 keyframe grid from the master at 15s intervals, (6) mastering_report.json — detected sync offsets, applied corrections, and ffprobe-measured stream inventory for all outputs.
